title: OPINION: Alaska’s next governor must be able to lead amid national turmoil
summary: This column is intended to inform, not inflame, and to connect national instability to practical decision-making in Alaska’s 2026 gubernatorial election. title: Crum deviated from state law and policy when investing Alaska’s savings, review finds
summary: Adam Crum speaks to reporters on Oct. 4, 2022 at the Alaska Scientific Crime Detection Lab in Anchorage while serving as health commissioner. (Wesley Early | Alaska Public Media) Former Alaska Revenue Commissioner Adam Crum deviated from state policy and failed to perform the necessary due diligence before committing millions in state savings to a private equity fund, according to an outside review ordered by Gov. title: The Empty Office Podcast – Season 4, Episode 2
summary: Senator Tobin and Mike Mason are joined by Gunnar Knapp, Professor Emeritus of Economics at the the University of Alaska Anchorage, for a conversation about the economy, fisheries, and public radio. title: Alaska drug overdose deaths drop, though less dramatically than national plunge
summary: Jeff Toole and Bernadette Hartley help assemble kits containing naloxone, a drug that reverses opioid overdoses, at a Aug. 29, 2025 event in Anchorage. The volunteer event held at the Fairview Community Recreation Center was organized by the Alaska Department of Health’s Project HOPE and the Alaska Native Tribal Health Consortium. Wider distribution of the naloxone kits may have contributed to a decreate in overdose deaths. (Yereth Rosen | Alaska Beacon) Alaska had fewer overdose deaths in 2024 than in the year prior...

title: Trump administration to acquire 10% of USA Rare Earth in $1.6B deal
summary: The US government plans to acquire a 10% stake in USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) as part of a $1.6 billion investment package to support the company’s development of a domestic mine-to-magnet supply chain, according to reports. title: Novagold’s Donlin costs, net value jump in update
summary: Novagold Resources (TSX: NG) says inflationary pressures mean its Donlin Gold project in Alaska will probably cost $9.2 billion – a 25% increase – while the value of the proposed mine has soared 66% on the back of higher commodity prices. title: Legislature convenes in Juneau

title: AST Arrest: Palmer [AK26007384]
summary: On 01/25/2026 at approximately 0125 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a report suspicious behavior in Palmer. Following the investigation Isaiah Homan, age 24 of Anchorage, was arrested for DUI. Homan was remanded to Mat-Su Pretrial where he is held with a bail of $500.00.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DUI: Fairbanks [AK26007378]
summary: On 1/24/2025, At approximately 2345 hours, Troopers Stopped a vehicle for multiple equipment violations. The driver Nathan Gregory (21) exhibited signs of impairment by alcohol. A DUI investigation revealed that he was Impaired while driving. He was arrested and remanded to FCC.
